label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out why Doyle was surprised. The question is asking what caused his surprise when the taxi driver addressed him by name. So, the story says that Doyle was in Europe, got out of the station, and got into a taxi. The driver immediately turned to him and asked where to take him, using his name: "Mr. Doyle." That's pretty surprising because Doyle had never met the driver before. The driver then explains how he deduced that Doyle was Arthur Conan Doyle. He mentions the newspaper story about Doyle's vacation in Marseilles, the taxi stop being where people returning from there come, Doyle's skin color suggesting a vacation, ink spot on his fingers implying a writer, and his English clothes. Then he concludes that Doyle must be Sir Arthur Conan Doyle. Doyle reacts with surprise, exclaiming that the driver is a real-life version of Sherlock Holmes. The driver then adds that the name on the suitcase also helped. The question is asking why Doyle was surprised. The options are: A) the driver had seen him before B) the driver could call him by his name C) the driver was waiting for him D) the driver answered no questions from him Looking at the story, the key point is that the driver used his deductive reasoning to figure out who Doyle was and called him by his name without being introduced. The driver didn't know him before, so option A is not correct. The driver
